§ About

  • AboutThe long version
  • NowThis month
  • ColophonCredits
¶ Hello

I’m Your Name, accessibility lead at Superhuman (formerly known as Grammarly). I spend most of my time thinking about what happens when you treat design as a form of care — for the people using the software, for the engineers building it, and for the systems that hold it all together.

For years my online presence was rented space — social profiles and digital CVs on platforms I didn’t own. This site is me building a home on the internet instead. The foundation is my own, the creaky floorboards are ones I coded, and the front door is always open.

It’s a place to slow down and share ideas that invite reflection rather than consumption — on design systems, accessibility, leadership, and whatever I’m paying attention to now.

Read the essays See what I’m up to now
on the desk, april ’26 →

§ Building

Prism — a perceptual contrast checker. Alpha with four teams.

§ Reading

The Craftsman, second pass. Design Justice, slowly.

§ Study 01 January 2026 · 8 min

Your first study

Studies are shorter than essays — typically a single investigated question with a small data set or a focused observation.

Tag one Study
¶ Notes

From the notebook

All notes ↗
  • Jan 01

    A note is a short fragment — usually one paragraph, sometimes two. The first paragraph becomes the lede on the reader page; subsequent paragraphs become the afterthought.

    where it was written
  • Jan 02

    Add as many MDX files as you like to src/content/notes/. They render in file order — keep the numeric 01-, 02- prefix and the most recent at the bottom (or invert the convention; build.js sorts by filename).

    another scribbled-on day
